title: Palindrome Permutation II
output_format: JSON array of all unique palindromic permutations.
constraints: s consists of lower-case English letters.
explanation: Count character frequencies. If more than one character has an odd frequency, impossible. Place the single odd character (if exists) in the middle. Then recursively backtrack half the remaining characters symmetrically to build permutations.
answer:
def solve(s):
    import collections
    counts = collections.Counter(s)
    odds = [c for c, count in counts.items() if count % 2 != 0]
    if len(odds) > 1: return '[]'
    mid = odds[0] if odds else ''
    half = []
    for c, count in counts.items():
        half.extend([c] * (count // 2))
    half.sort()
    res = []
    def backtrack(path, used):
        if len(path) == len(half):
            p = "".join(path)
            res.append("\"" + p + mid + p[::-1] + "\"")
            return
        for i in range(len(half)):
            if used[i]: continue
            if i > 0 and half[i] == half[i-1] and not used[i-1]: continue
            used[i] = True
            path.append(half[i])
            backtrack(path, used)
            path.pop()
            used[i] = False
    backtrack([], [False]*len(half))
    return '[' + ', '.join(res) + ']'

import sys

def solve(s):
    pass

if __name__ == '__main__':
    lines = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
    s = lines[0].strip() if lines else ""
    print(solve(s))
